evidence_quotes.quote: video game protections from nefarious publishers
titles which I have enjoyed in the past that I legally purchased and owned are no longer playable.
many more games which became unplayable or lacking in functionality due to lack of support or mismanagement of DRM functionalities.

paragraphs.text: I believe the DFA would benefit from including video game protections from nefarious publishers as it would very well fit the goal of this Act. Sadly, may titles which I have enjoyed in the past that I legally purchased and owned are no longer playable. One major publisher who is notorious for "pulling the rug" on these games in Ubisoft: The Crew, XDefiant, and many more games which became unplayable or lacking in functionality due to lack of support or mismanagement of DRM functionalities. No matter who the bad actor happens to be, the customer should have a right to preserve what he owns digitally. Developers should have the duty to leave options to consumers when it comes to preserving the experience that they paid for on their own. I hope this may come to pass, sincerely.
